mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and next-20-day SPX returns is below 0.05 over a 3-year backtest, or if periods of above-median RSAFS growth do not yield significantly positive mean returns (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 7%, it signals labor market slack that depresses aggregate demand and corporate profit expectations, pushing S&P 500 lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day excess return following UNRATE > 7% triggers is less negative than -1% (i.e., > -1%) or if the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with declining retail sales (RSAFS) jointly indicate that monetary tightening is extinguishing consumer demand, amplifying the negative impact on equity valuations over the next month compared to either signal alone.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (FEDFUNDS * RSAFS_growth) in a linear regression predicting 20-day SPX returns has a p-value above 0.20 or if the incremental predictive power (ΔR²) of the interaction over main effects is less than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During an industrial expansion regime (sustained INDPRO growth), higher industrial production signals rising capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If the strategy that goes long SPX when INDPRO is above its 6-month median fails to produce a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 over a 5-day holding period, or if the win rate is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.5%, it indicates the end of the extreme low-rate environment, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day return following FEDFUNDS > 0.5% triggers is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10), or if the hit rate of negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a self-reinforcing cycle of employment and housing demand that boosts economic activity and corporate earnings, lifting S&P 500 over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the composite signal (standardized sum of PAYEMS and HOUST growth) has a Rank IC below 0.04 over a 3-year walk-forward, or if its performance does not exceed that of either single series individually.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong manufacturing activity, which directly contributes to GDP growth and supports equity prices; this signal is always active and predicts positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns are not positively correlated with INDPRO growth in a linear regression (beta coefficient not significantly >0 at 10% level), or if the Hit Ratio for positive returns when INDPRO is above its 12-month moving average is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP (GDPC1) quarterly growth exceeds 2% annualized, the economy is expanding at a solid pace, raising corporate earnings expectations and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day return following GDP growth > 2% triggers is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p < 0.10), or if the hit rate is below 55%.
